qa_pairs.question: How is RAP membership represented?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership is represented as a group in LDAP.

qa_pairs.question: What does RAP membership define for users?
qa_pairs.answer: RAP membership defines a group of users that are authorized to submit jobs against the RAPI (which is the ID of the RAP) and share files within the same Unix group.

qa_pairs.question: When a user submits a job to the scheduler, to which allocation is it assigned?
qa_pairs.answer: When a user submits a job to the scheduler, the job will be assigned to the current allocation of the user's Principal Investigator (PI).

qa_pairs.question: What must a user do if they have more than one Principal Investigator (PI) when submitting a job?
qa_pairs.answer: If a user has more than one PI (i.e., more than one group), they must specify a group name as the value of the `--account` option.
